serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Ribena Less Sugar is approximately 250 ml (one standard drinking glass), providing about 85 kcal and 20.5 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Ribena (less sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ribena (less sugar), record ID D03070033. All per-100g values are as reported.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fruit juices and fruit drinks — University of Sydney GI Database; blackcurrant drink / fruit juice drink analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Ribena Less Sugar in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: commercial fruit juice drinks with similar sugar content (8–10 g/100 ml) which cluster in the GI 60–70 range (medium). Glycemic load per 250 ml serving = (65 × 8.4 × 2.5) / 100 = 13.65, rounded to 13.7. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement if available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.2 g sugar per 100 ml; dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Ribena Less Sugar because its 8.2 g/100 ml sugar content exceeds the 5 g/100 ml SSB threshold and dietary sugar independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite the negligible sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: 8.2 g free sugar per 100 ml with zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ribena Less Sugar is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes because it delivers 8.2 g of free sugar per 100 ml in liquid form with no fibre buffer, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; a zero-sugar or genuinely unsweetened blackcurrant alternative should be chosen instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased CVD risk through triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance pathways
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit Ribena Less Sugar because habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption raises fasting triglycerides and promotes insulin resistance, both independent CVD risk factors, even at the reduced sugar level of 8.2 g/100 ml.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dietary sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ribena Less Sugar is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis because its 8.2 g/100 ml sugar content promotes inflammatory mediators and the beverage provides no offsetting anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3, polyphenols, or fibre.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ribena Less Sugar is a commercially manufactured blackcurrant-flavoured fruit juice drink produced by Suntory (formerly GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Healthcare), widely sold in Singapore and across Asia-Pacific. It is a reduced-sugar variant of the classic Ribena blackcurrant cordial, formulated to contain less added sugar than the original while retaining the characteristic deep-purple blackcurrant flavour and slight tartness. The drink is typically sold ready-to-drink in cartons or bottles and is consumed chilled. Per 100 ml, it provides 34 kcal, 8.4 g carbohydrate (of which 8.2 g is sugar), and negligible protein, fat, fibre, and sodium. Despite the 'less sugar' labelling, the product remains a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) by nutritional classification because its sugar content of 8.2 g/100 ml is well above zero. Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its free-sugar load and absence of fibre, which results in rapid postprandial glucose elevation. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 13.7 per 250 ml serving. It is plant-based, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Consumers seeking a health-appropriate alternative should choose Ribena No Added Sugar (0 g sugar) or unsweetened blackcurrant beverages.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics drink Ribena less sugar?
How much sugar is in Ribena less sugar?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (exact match, record D03070033). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework for commercial fruit juice drinks of similar sugar content; confidence for GI component is medium, but overall record confidence is high given exact nutrient provenance.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — this is a sugar-water-based fruit drink containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — the product contains no fat-bearing ingredients by formulation. saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — the product is a clarified juice drink with no intact fibre. sodium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo. Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for this specific commercial product is not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. GI note: GI value of 65 is an analogous estimate based on commercial fruit juice drinks with similar sugar content (8–10 g/100 ml) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Ribena Less Sugar was found in approved sources. GL per 250 ml serving calculated as (65 × 8.4 × 2.5) / 100 = 13.65, rounded to 13.7. Multilingual names: not applicable — Ribena is a global commercial brand with no cuisine-specific local-language name requirement; English aliases are comprehensive.
